message:
feat(websocket): add send_bytes/ping/close convenience API and fix close handshake receive

core/websocket.rs:
- Add send_bytes() for sending binary messages without manually
  constructing Frame structs
- Add ping() with MAX_CONTROL_PAYLOAD_BYTES (125) validation
- Add close() that accepts an explicit close code and optional
  reason string, building the payload via new build_close_payload()
  helper which truncates the reason on a UTF-8 character boundary
  to stay within the 125-byte control frame limit
- Extract MAX_CONTROL_PAYLOAD_BYTES (125) and MAX_CLOSE_REASON_BYTES
  (123) constants, replacing the magic number in send_pong()
- Add tests: build_close_payload_rejects_invalid_code verifies that
  reserved code 1006 is rejected; build_close_payload_truncates_on_
  utf8_boundary verifies multi-byte reason strings are truncated
  without splitting codepoints

http/websocket.rs:
- Fix receive() to allow reading after sending a Close frame by
  adding ensure_can_receive() that permits WsState::CloseSent,
  which is required by RFC 6455 Section 5.5.1: after sending Close,
  the endpoint should continue reading until it receives the peer's
  Close response. Previously, ensure_open() rejected CloseSent,
  making a clean close handshake impossible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
